$comment: ELF64 EXECUTABLE (ET_EXEC) for AArch64 Linux. Ships at the D-LK6-1 sibling cycle (2026-06-01) — the closed-enum RelocFormulaKind reshape unblocked ARM64 reloc formula application; intra-module ET_EXEC works via the shared `applyExecRelocations` kernel. Consumed by `src/link/format/elf.cpp`. Self-contained scope: extern imports + PLT stub bytes are anchored at D-LK6-8 (machine-specific PLT — ARM64 needs ADRP+LDR+BR 16-byte sequence vs x86_64's 6-byte `FF 25 disp32`); until that lands, dynamic linking on ARM64 fails loud. Static self-contained execs with intra-module relocations work today.
$entryPointComment: Empty entryPoint defaults the walker to module.functions[0] (cycle-2 convention). Once D-LK1-1 closes (real symbol-name thread through HIR→LIR→AssembledFunction), this field will name the entry symbol and the walker will resolve it by name.
$processExitComment: D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 (v0.0.2 V2-1): the Linux AArch64 runnable-binary spine — a raw `exit_group` syscall (no libc dependency). The trampoline emitter loads the syscall number into x8 via `mov x8, 94` (the new MOVZ Imm16 encoding — see arm64.target.json), moves the user fn's return value from returnGprs[0] (x0) into argGprs[0] (x0 — a no-op mov here since both are x0, emitted for ABI uniformity with x86_64's rax→rdi), then issues SVC #0 (the target's `syscall` opcode). Linux AArch64 syscall ABI: x8 = syscall number, x0 = arg0 (exit status). syscallOpcodeBytes [1, 0, 0, 212] = the LITTLE-ENDIAN byte stream of 0xD4000001 (SVC #0); it cross-checks the target's `syscall` opcode fixedWord. Self-contained exit mechanism — no ADRP+LDR+BLR `call_indirect_via_extern` is needed (that GOT/PLT path stays deferred at D-LK6-8).
$entryCallingConventionComment: AAPCS64 calling convention — the user fn's return value (returnGprs[0] = x0) is moved into argGprs[0] (= x0) before SVC. Read via `target.callingConventionByName("aapcs64")` at trampoline-build time.

elf.$comment: Elf64_Ehdr identity for an executable. type = ET_EXEC (2). class = ELFCLASS64; data = ELFDATA2LSB; osabi = sysv; machine = EM_AARCH64 (183). pageAlign = 4096 — most ARM64 Linux distributions ship with 4 KB pages. Apple Silicon (16 KB) and high-end ARM64 server configs (64 KB) declare their own values in their respective schemas; the same kernel PT_LOAD congruence rule (`p_vaddr % p_align == p_offset % p_align`) applies uniformly.
elf.$bindNowComment: Eager binding — same v1 stance as x86_64 ELF (plan 14 §5 risk row). Emits DT_FLAGS_1 = DF_1_NOW so dyld resolves every GOT slot at load. Lazy upgrade anchored at D-LK6-11.

sections.$comment: Executable code section. virtualAddress = 0x400000 — ARM64 Linux convention (Linux loaders place ARM64 ET_EXEC at the same low base as x86_64 by default; AArch64 ELF psABI does not mandate a specific base). sh_flags = SHF_ALLOC | SHF_EXECINSTR (6); sh_addralign = 16 (cache-friendly; ARM64 instructions are 4-byte but section alignment is conventionally larger).
sections.name: .text

sections.$comment: Symbol table — conventional for debugger support. sh_type = SHT_SYMTAB (2); sh_addralign = 8; sh_entsize = 24 (Elf64_Sym).
sections.name: .symtab
